summary:
• Middle East geopolitical tensions between Iran and the U.S. are creating persistent upward pressure on crude prices, with analysts warning that any disruption to the Strait of Hormuz could extend supply constraints through year-end.

• Argentina's Vaca Muerta shale production faces regulatory approval delays for major development projects, potentially constraining Western Hemisphere crude supply growth despite industry investment commitments.

• Refinery capacity constraints and shifting energy infrastructure priorities are limiting downstream processing capacity, as producers pivot toward renewable fuel production rather than expanding conventional oil refining.
